overview: Mono-Blue Faeries is a versatile Pauper archetype that leverages Ninjutsu interactions to generate massive value and control the board. It adapts between aggressive and controlling playstyles depending on the matchup, utilizing bounce effects and card draw to outmaneuver opponents. The deck is highly effective against combo strategies like High Tide while remaining competitive against midrange and aggro decks.
win_conditions: Repeatedly Ninjutsu creatures using bounce effects like Shrieking Drake
Attacking with unblockable Ninjas protected by Aqueous Form to draw cards and create value
Countering key opponent spells to stall and outvalue them

keep_criteria: Hands with a Ninja and backup lands/cards for turn 2
Hands without Ninjas against combo decks like High Tide
Keep hands with 2–3 lands, at least one turn-1 or turn-2 play (Spellstutter Sprite, Delver of Secrets, or card draw), and at least one interaction spell (Snap, Counterspell, or Spell Pierce).
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early Ninjas against aggressive or midrange decks
Hands that cannot establish board presence or interact early
Mulligan if you have no lands, all spells costing 3+ mana, or zero interaction against aggro/combo decks.

examples.reason: Can play a Ninja on turn 2 with backup and develop game plan; Spellstutter deals with wide array of spells.
Specific keep only against High Tide and similar combo decks.
Great opening hand; allows proactive play against slow decks or responsive play against dangerous decks.

early_game: Play a Ninja on turn 2 if possible
Assess matchup to play proactively or responsively
Use counters like Spellstutter or Counterspell to disrupt early threats
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Spellstutter Sprite or Delver of Secrets.
Turn 2: Cast Snap or Counterspell, potentially flashing in Spellstutter Sprite to counter a key spell or protect itself.
Sequence lands to guarantee blue mana on turns 1 and 2.
If holding a hand with multiple cheap spells, prioritize establishing a board presence while saving interaction for the opponent’s first meaningful play.
mid_game: Utilize Shrieking Drake to bounce Ninjas and repeat Ninjutsu loops
Cast Aqueous Form on a Ninja to attack freely and draw cards
Counter key removal or board wipes to protect your board

Hoards counterspells for key threats, uses flash creatures to protect combo pieces or counter removal, and manages the graveyard carefully (Snapcaster Mage needs it, but avoid giving it to opponents).
Don’t overcommit mana if the opponent has interaction
preserve card advantage for late-game value.
Commit threats when you have interaction to protect them or when the opponent is tapped out.
Hold back if they have sweepers or heavy removal.
Use flash to respond to their plays and maintain board control without overextending.
late_game: Transition to control by countering finishers and stalling
Generate massive value through repeated Ninjutsu and scry effects
Punish opponents who overextend or eat more than they can chew
Delver of Secrets transforming into Inverter of Truth (with Chalice of the Void), Spellstutter Sprite + Snap/Counterspell + Spell Queller/Inverter, or grinding with card advantage and flash threats.
Go all-in when you have card advantage, the opponent is tapped out, or you’ve established board control with counterspells.
Commit if you can protect your finisher or if the opponent has no interaction left.

key_cards.name: Shrieking Drake
key_cards.notes: Lets you Ninjutsu infinitely by bouncing creatures; interacts well with Harrier Strix and Faerie Seer; acts as pseudo-Faerie Miscreant.

key_cards.name: Aqueous Form
key_cards.notes: Makes Ninja unblockable and draws a card with Scry 1; ideally used on Ninja of the Deep-Hours.

key_cards.name: Ninja of the Deep-Hours
key_cards.notes: Core creature for Ninjutsu loops and Aqueous Form synergy.

key_cards.name: Counterspell
key_cards.notes: Critical for disrupting opponent strategies, especially against Golgari Gardens and Bogles.

key_cards.name: Harrier Strix
key_cards.notes: Provides bounce effect and scry to find Drake or Ninjas; sideboarded out against some matchups.

sequencing_tips: Wait for Gigadrowse to resolve to untap lands and save mana for a counter
Use Shrieking Drake's bounce after Ninjutsu to return the Ninja to hand for reuse
Play counters at the right time to control opponent rather than wasting them early
Adjust aggression based on opponent's removal density and deck speed
Common mistakes include overcommitting without protection, mismanaging Snapcaster Mage’s graveyard, and forgetting that Spellstutter Sprite’s counter reduces as you cast more spells.
Unique interactions: Sprite + Snap/Counterspell can counter multiple spells in a chain.
Always track opponent’s mana and graveyard to maximize your interaction.

matchups.advice: Prevent opponent from drawing cards at the right time and force them to play aggressively. Tread carefully between aggro and control strategies.
Play around their removals and don't worry too much about their creatures. Post-side, use tools to punish overextension.
Defined by who draws better and is more assertive. Use Shrieking Drake to bounce extra cards and reuse Brinebarrow Intruder. Side in Relic of Progenitus and Unable to Scream.
Prevent opponent from using High Tide and protect lands from Gigadrowse. Snap or wait for Gigadrowse to resolve. Use their High Tides for your own mana.
Deal with main strategies and create value from opponent. Counter Ethereal Armor and Armadillo Cloak only if beneficial. Use combat tricks and counters wisely.
Use cheap spells (Pierce, Sprite) to probe, cast multiple spells in a row to overwhelm counters, use card draw to out-value, and sideboard in cheaper interaction or alternative win conditions.

Deploy early interaction (Sprite, Pierce), Chalice of the Void on 1 or 2, counterspells for key ramp spells, and stabilize with flash threats. Disrupt their mana acceleration before they can cast their game-ending spells.

Use early counterspells, flash threats to trade efficiently, Daze/Force of Will if available, and sideboard in more removal/counter. Don’t overextend; stabilize by turn 3–4 and close out the game with evasion.

Apply pressure with evasion, use card advantage to out-grind, protect your combo pieces, and sideboard in more interaction to break their control shell. Force them to spend resources on your threats rather than their own game plan.

Win the interaction war. Hold counters for their key spells, use Spell Queller to protect your threats, win the card advantage race, and sideboard in more Negate/Queller. Play conservatively early and capitalize on their missteps.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (Wrath of God, Day of Judgment), heavy removal (Path to Exile, Lightning Bolt), combo pieces (Elves’ Genesis, Affinity’s Arcbound Ravager), and control finishers (Teferi, Liliana).

overview_evidence.evidence_span: One of the best things about Mono-U is how it can go from aggro to control depending on the situation.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Drake lets you Ninjutsu an infinite number of times, as its ability lets you bounce a creature.
This card lets you attack with your Ninja freely... and draw a card with Scry 1, which is what makes this entire interaction extremely powerful.
Faeries has a certain advantage over them because of Counterspell.

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: You can also wait for Gigadrowse to resolve to untap your lands and save some mana to play a counter.
You can play it on the board after using Ninjutsu, return the Ninja in question to your hand, and then use Ninjutsu on the Drake on the following turn.
Use your combat tricks well, and play your counters at the right time to control your opponent.
Against dangerous decks, you can play more passively, more responsively instead of proactively.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: The most dangerous thing in this matchup are flying creatures and the incredible recursion Rakdos Madness has with Sneaky Snacker.
Refurbished Familiar can be annoying, and Crypt Rats is incredible against Faeries, as it can clear the board very easily.
Any deck with Kor Skyfisher should be an issue for Faeries, right?
Be careful with Summon: Choco/Mog , as it can end the game very quickly if the board is full.
prevent them from tapping your lands with Gigadrowse so you can play your combo freely.
Ethereal Armor and Armadillo Cloak are the auras you want to counter
